summary: Renamed a set of anonymous assembly labels and func_XXXXXXXX placeholders to more meaningful sysdolphin, AX, exception, devcom, and synth function names, and updated callsites plus include/functions.h comment declarations accordingly. The PR was a label/prototype cleanup rather than a C decompilation change; it improved cross-file readability for audio synthesis callbacks, device communication callbacks, AX callback registration, and exception-reporting helpers.
what_changed: Renamed AX function func_80359D84 to AXRegisterCallback and updated the HSD_SynthInit callsite to branch to AXRegisterCallback.

Renamed exception-related functions func_80394A48, func_80394B18, and func_80397DF4 to Exception_ReportStackTrace, Exception_ReportCodeline, and Exception_StoreDebugLevel, then updated debug code callsites in asm/melee/db/code_80225374.s.
Renamed devcom function func_8038EA50 to HSD_DevComIsBusy and updated a polling loop in asm/melee/text_2.s to call the named function.

Added global labels for devcom callback entry points, including HSD_DevComDVDMemCallback and HSD_DevComDVDCallback, and updated address materialization operands that previously referenced lbl_8038F210 and lbl_8038F2F0.
Renamed synth audio mixing functions func_80389D24 and func_8038A600 to HSD_SynthSFXSetMix and HSD_SynthSFXUpdateMix, and updated internal synth.s and AXDriver.s callsites.

Added or promoted synth callback labels including dropcallback, HSD_SynthPStreamFirstHakoHeaderCallback, and HSD_SynthPStreamHeaderCallback, then updated callsites and function-pointer address loads to use the new labels.
Updated include/functions.h comment declarations so the known symbol list uses the new names instead of func_XXXXXXXX placeholders.
smart_moves: The PR consistently updated both symbol definitions and every visible branch or address-load reference in the diff, avoiding stale references to old lbl_ or func_ names.

For callback functions passed by address, the patch renamed both the function label and the lis/addi @ha/@l operands, which is important because callback identities are often recovered from function-pointer setup rather than direct branches.
The naming grouped functions under existing library-style prefixes such as HSD_Synth, HSD_DevCom, AX, and Exception, making subsystem ownership searchable and easier to infer.
The include/functions.h updates kept the central symbol list synchronized with assembly labels, reducing future churn when these functions are moved toward typed prototypes or C decompilation.
decomp_lessons: When a placeholder function is identified, update all direct branch callsites and all function-pointer construction sites in assembly, not just the .global definition.
Callback labels are worth naming early because they explain why otherwise anonymous address loads exist and help reconstruct API relationships for later C work.
Use subsystem prefixes consistently: HSD_Synth* for baselib synth functions, HSD_DevCom* for devcom functions, AX* for dolphin AX APIs, and Exception_* for exception/debug helpers.
Even comment-only declarations in include/functions.h act as a project-wide index of known symbols; keep them aligned with assembly labels.
Promoting local lbl_ labels to .global names can be useful when a function has externally meaningful callback semantics, even before its signature is known.
naming_conventions: HSD-prefixed names were used for sysdolphin baselib functions; Exception_ names used an underscore-separated module prefix.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Pure assembly label replacement: .global declarations and label names were changed without altering instruction bytes or control flow.
Direct branch comments were updated from old func_ names to new symbol names, e.g. bl HSD_SynthSFXUpdateMix and bl Exception_ReportStackTrace.
Function-pointer materialization using lis/addi with @ha/@l was retargeted from anonymous labels to named callback symbols, e.g. dropcallback@ha/dropcallback@l and HSD_DevComDVDCallback@ha/@l.
Named callback entry points were made global where needed so subsequent assembly references and future decompilation units can refer to symbolic names.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for typed prototypes or C implementations of AXRegisterCallback, HSD_DevComIsBusy, and HSD_SynthSFXUpdateMix.
Check whether dropcallback was later renamed to a more subsystem-prefixed HSD_Synth name.
Look for future cleanup of Exception_ReportStackTrace, Exception_ReportCodeline, and Exception_StoreDebugLevel signatures.
Audit remaining sysdolphin baselib func_8038 and lbl_8038 callback labels for similar naming opportunities.
